message:
fix(printf): route u64 > i64::MAX through PrintfArg::Ulong (bd-vgrav)

parse_printf_args() now recognizes JSON integers that overflow i64
and bucketed them into a new PrintfArg::Ulong(c_ulonglong) variant
rather than silently rounding through f64. Dispatch arms for
[Ulong] added to run_impl_snprintf, run_host_snprintf,
run_impl_fprintf, run_host_fprintf. Removes sprintf_llu_max
(%llu + ULLONG_MAX) from KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-11nb","title":"bd-qwm subtask: Phase-0 startup hardening and host delegation boundary correctness","description":"Background:
- Current startup phase-0 path needs hardening and explicit fallback guarantees under real interposition conditions.

Goal:
- Harden phase-0 startup implementation and host-libc delegation boundaries.

Deliverables:
1) Robust argument/environment/auxv validation path.
2) Deterministic fallback to host startup when phase-0 is not enabled/safe.
3) Invariant capture snapshot updates for debugging and auditability.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Phase-0 path runs safely for controlled fixtures.
- Delegation path remains reliable for normal LD_PRELOAD operation.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for validator/fallback branches.
- Integration tests for phase-0 opt-in and fallback scenarios.
- Structured logs with startup decision trace and artifact refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:53.330234447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:13:35.562025694Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T04:13:35.562004214Z","close_reason":"Completed startup hardening/delegation implementation + tests; workspace-wide clippy/test gates remain blocked by unrelated pre-existing failures outside bd-11nb.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","e2e","frankenlibc","implementation","logging","math","pthread","startup","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":625,"issue_id":"bd-11nb","author":"PurplePond","text":"Implementation update (PurplePond): Hardened phase-0 startup boundary in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/startup_abi.rs by (1) enforcing membrane deny short-circuit with explicit MembraneDenied failure reason, (2) adding deterministic host delegation fallback matrix for phase-0 unsafe denials (unterminated argv/envp/auxv + membrane-denied) when __libc_start_main is in phase0 mode, and (3) adding debug test-host delegate override hook for deterministic delegation tests. Expanded integration coverage in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/startup_abi_contract_test.rs with new __libc_start_main tests: phase0-disabled delegates to host, phase0-unsafe envp scan falls back to host, and missing-main remains deny/no fallback. Added structured startup trace artifact writer at target/conformance/bd-11nb_startup_phase0.log.jsonl (trace_id prefix bd-11nb::startup::NNN).\n\nVerification commands run:\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test startup_abi_contract_test (PASS, 11 tests)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (FAIL due pre-existing unrelated clippy violations in frankenlibc-membrane pressure_sensor.rs + fragmentation_storms_test.rs and frankenlibc-core allocator/rcu modules)\n- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets (FAIL due pre-existing unrelated pthread_mutex_core_test failures outside bd-11nb scope)\n- cargo fmt --check currently fails workspace-wide due pre-existing formatting drift in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/pressure_sensor.rs unrelated to bd-11nb edits.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:06:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11sh","title":"syscall: pidfd_open invalid pid should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for pidfd_open(-1, 0). Add ABI regression coverage so the invalid-pid contract for pidfd_open is explicit and exact.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:33.755436676Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:21:40.466035018Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:21:40.463218764Z","close_reason":"Added pidfd_open_invalid_pid_sets_einval_like_host asserting exact EINVAL for pidfd_open(-1, 0).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-122j","title":"bd-1gh subtask: pthread_getspecific/pthread_setspecific thread-local correctness","description":"Background:
- getspecific/setspecific semantics are core to thread-local correctness and must integrate with thread bootstrap/TLS.

Goal:
- Implement pthread_getspecific/pthread_setspecific with deterministic thread-local isolation.

Deliverables:
1) Per-thread key-value lookup/store path.
2) Null/default behavior and key validity checks.
3) Concurrency safety under thread churn.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Cross-thread isolation is guaranteed in tests.
- Error behaviors match declared support semantics.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for set/get across many keys and threads.
- E2E tests with mixed thread operations.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, key_id, op, outcome, errno.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.511446598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:44.096040118Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:44.095946433Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":626,"issue_id":"bd-122j","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"PurpleHawk: getspecific/setspecific implementation verified. Per-thread key-value lookup is allocation-free via TID→values-ptr table. Null/default behavior correct (returns 0 for unset keys). Key validity checked against global registry. Tests added covering: roundtrip, invalid/deleted key rejection, 64-key independence, overwrite, zero-value handling, cross-thread isolation via registered blocks, out-of-bounds boundary. 29 passing TLS tests total.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:43Z"}]}

Test is green end-to-end; remaining impl gaps (negative star, length modifiers, u64 overflow, %n FORTIFY) are surfaced as individual beads (bd-0u5fm, bd-luc3d, bd-vgrav, bd-qij6l) via KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS skip list with tracking comments.  {"id":"bd-130","title":"Replacement profile guard: enforce zero glibc pthread/syscall call-through","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Add CI gate that fails replacement build if forbidden call-through symbols appear.
- Maintain allowlist for interpose profile only.

Acceptance:
- Gate reports exact offending symbol + file when violated.
- Replacement profile can be certified as no-callthrough for covered primitives.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.554752662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:34:01.650198Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:34:01.650198Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","pthread","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-130","depends_on_id":"bd-c1x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-130","depends_on_id":"bd-cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-130","depends_on_id":"bd-ef2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-130","depends_on_id":"bd-yos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-130","depends_on_id":"bd-z84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":627,"issue_id":"bd-130","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Profile definition: tests/conformance/replacement_profile.json
- Defines interpose vs replacement profiles
- Interpose allowlist: 11 modules permitted to call through
- Detection rules for distinguishing call-throughs from raw syscalls/constants/types
- Call-through census: 86 total across 11 modules
- Modules: pthread_abi (17), socket_abi (14), unistd_abi (24), termios_abi (10), dlfcn_abi (4), signal_abi (4), io_abi (4), time_abi (3), resource_abi (3), dirent_abi (2), process_abi (1)

### 2. CI gate: scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh
4 checks:
- Profile definition exists
- Call-through scan (interpose: pass if allowlisted; replacement: zero tolerance)
- Pthread isolation (no pthread calls outside pthread_abi.rs)
- Raw syscall audit (shows safe syscall sites)

Usage: bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh [interpose|replacement]
- interpose mode: PASS (all 86 call-throughs in allowlisted modules)
- replacement mode: FAIL (86 violations; expected until modules are reimplemented)

### 3. Integration tests: crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/replacement_guard_test.rs
7 tests:
- profile_exists_and_valid
- guard_script_exists_and_executable
- interpose_allowlist_covers_all_call_through_modules
- no_pthread_calls_outside_pthread_abi
- call_through_census_matches_reality
- replacement_profile_has_both_modes
- raw_syscalls_are_not_flagged

### Test commands
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test replacement_guard_test
bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh interpose
bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh replacement

All 7 tests pass. CI gate passes in interpose mode. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:34:01.650198Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-13n0","title":"io_uring kernel ABI struct layout conformance tests","description":"Cod added IoUringSqringOffsets/IoUringCqringOffsets/IoUringParams structs (commits 5ac4e62/ce66934) with a size_of IoUringParams==120 assertion. Missing: (1) size assertions for IoUringSqringOffsets and IoUringCqringOffsets (both 40 bytes per kernel io_uring.h); (2) field-offset assertions locking down the ABI layout so a field reorder or padding regression fails at compile-/test-time rather than corrupting kernel interop silently. Spec source: Linux include/uapi/linux/io_uring.h. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:04:27.104862444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:08:48.453680186Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:08:48.451695730Z","close_reason":"Added offset_of! layout assertions (23 field offsets) + IORING_ENTER_* flag-bit pins in commit d378390. Freezes kernel ABI layout for IoUringSqringOffsets, IoUringCqringOffsets, and IoUringParams per Linux include/uapi/linux/io_uring.h. Complements cod's size_of assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-s39o.3","title":"Test and fix network diagnostic tools under LD_PRELOAD (wget, dig, nslookup)","description":"Test wget, dig, nslookup, host under LD_PRELOAD.
These exercise: DNS resolution (getaddrinfo beyond /etc/hosts), socket connect, SSL/TLS, HTTP parsing.
Our getaddrinfo only supports numeric hosts and /etc/hosts lookups. For DNS resolution, we'd need to either:
1. Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric hosts
2. Implement a DNS resolver stub
Option 1 is more practical for now.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31.400999331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:30.152849132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:16.988805266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.277849234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-s39o.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"wget resolves DNS, connects, but GnuTLS has thread locking error (separate SSL issue). curl works perfectly including HTTPS. dig/nslookup are statically linked (can't preload). host works.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vfl","title":"Docs: align README/FEATURE_PARITY with support taxonomy (no overclaim)","description":"Critique mapping: #2 + bottom-line.

Deliverables:
- Update README to include an explicit truth table:
  - what this is today (interposition vs replacement)
  - which subsystems are still call-through
  - which are stubs
- Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to reflect the same classification.

Acceptance:
- No claim of full replacement unless supported by the matrix + E2E harness.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:46.293544458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767467115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767444111Z","close_reason":"Aligned README/FEATURE_PARITY to support taxonomy snapshot; removed full-replacement overclaims; added hybrid interposition truth table","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs"]}
 {"id":"bd-vfze","title":"sysvipc: shmdt(NULL) should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for shmdt(NULL). Tighten the ABI regression test from a broad errno family to an exact host-parity assertion so the detachment null-pointer contract cannot drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:25:54.992564574Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:28:00.696755061Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:28:00.694339928Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity shmdt(NULL) behavior: exact EINVAL instead of a broad SysV IPC errno family; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-vgrav","title":"printf_conformance executor misrepresents u64 > i64::MAX as Double","description":"parse_printf_args() treats JSON integers larger than i64::MAX as PrintfArg::Double because as_i64 fails and the Double branch catches 'abs > i64::MAX as f64'. For %llu with ULLONG_MAX the dispatch then goes to the [Double] arm and varargs type mismatch produces wrong output. Needs a PrintfArg::Ulong (or equivalent unsigned path) and an explicit u64 branch in parse_printf_args. Surfaced by sprintf_llu_max in bd-12hh matrix.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:33:34.390958382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:33:34.390958382Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-vgrav","title":"printf_conformance executor misrepresents u64 > i64::MAX as Double","description":"parse_printf_args() treats JSON integers larger than i64::MAX as PrintfArg::Double because as_i64 fails and the Double branch catches 'abs > i64::MAX as f64'. For %llu with ULLONG_MAX the dispatch then goes to the [Double] arm and varargs type mismatch produces wrong output. Needs a PrintfArg::Ulong (or equivalent unsigned path) and an explicit u64 branch in parse_printf_args. Surfaced by sprintf_llu_max in bd-12hh matrix.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:33:34.390958382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:29.385685109Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1147,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.

Also hardened scripts/perf_gate.sh for dev environments:
- skip under extreme system load (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED=1, GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR=0.85)
- optional kernel-suite run flag (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1) collecting RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines.

Baseline comparisons for kernel-suite metrics not yet enforced (needs baseline schema + stable host load to record p50s).","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:32:28Z"},{"id":1148,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. BrightMoose delivered per-kernel bench with 6 criterion groups: risk_upper_bound_ppm, bandit_select_profile, barrier_admissible, pareto_recommend_profile, design_choose_plan, approachability_observe/summary. Machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines for CI gating. Wired into Cargo.toml and perf_gate.sh (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1). Note: per-kernel baseline enforcement deferred to stable host load recording.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-x2sq","title":"bd-1rf subtask: NSS strict+hardened fixture/e2e closure with matrix drift checks","description":"Background:
- NSS backend completion must be reflected in conformance/e2e gates and support matrix truth.

Goal:
- Build strict/hardened NSS fixture pack and close evidence loop for supported files backend APIs.

Deliverables:
1) Fixture sets for passwd/group/hosts lookups.
2) E2E scenarios using integration binaries.
3) Support matrix and reality report consistency updates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- NSS fixture/e2e suite passes deterministically in target scope.
- Docs/matrix/report remain drift-free under guard scripts.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E scripts with comprehensive structured logs and artifact refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"2026-02-18 status (IcyCat): NSS fixture executor wiring completed in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs; NSS fixture packs expanded (pwd_ops/grp_ops/resolver); new integration fixture tests/integration/fixture_nss.c added and c_fixture_spec/link_test updated. Verification: cargo fmt --check PASS; rch cargo check/clippy PASS; rch cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance PASS (57 tests); rch bash scripts/check_c_fixture_suite.sh PASS. Blockers: workspace test hang in frankenlibc-core pthread::thread::tests::child_thread_can_write_shared_memory (tracked as bd-2o7i); global drift/maintenance gates failing due current repository-wide state; c_fixture_suite currently failing broadly and fixture_nss times out under preload though it passes without preload.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IcyCat","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.194463803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:51:55.211602975Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T23:51:55.211280986Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","nss","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-x2sq","depends_on_id":"bd-2yhf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-x2sq","depends_on_id":"bd-3ehb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-x2sq","depends_on_id":"bd-3rag","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-x2sq","depends_on_id":"bd-3vtx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-x66ds","title":"printf %g uses %e style for exponents -4..-2 (should use %f)","description":"crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs format_g() has wrong C11 threshold: 'if exp >= -(1) && exp < p as i32 {...%f...}'.
